sections.paragraph:
προαίρεσις, εως, ἡ (αἱρέω; Pla., Demosth., Aristot., ins, pap, LXX, Test12Patr, EpArist, Philo, Joseph., Just., Tat., Ath.) a choosing of one thing before another, choice, commitment (freq. w. a political connotation, of one who chooses the correct side and manifests loyalty, s. Welles, ind.; also a favorite term of moral philosophers, e.g. Diogenes 21, 5 p. 114 Malherbe) λ[ά]β̣ε̣τ̣ε̣ π̣[ροαίρ]|εσιν ἀγαθήν make the right commitment, that is, from polytheism to Christianity AcPl Ha 1, 20 (for the use of ἀγαθ. w. προαίρεσις s. Welles, no. 75, 10, of a treasurer who is commended for his ‘exemplary dedication’ or ‘commitment’ to his responsibilities); s. the ed’s. note on the lacuna in 2, 2f.—M-M s.v. προαιρέω.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Welles Welles = CW., Royal Correspondence in the Hellenistic Period—Lists 3, 6

ind. ind. = index

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Malherbe Malherbe = AM., various works

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 865
